# Beads (bd) - Distributed Graph Issue Tracker

Beads is a distributed graph issue tracker designed specifically for AI agents and multi-agent workflows, powered by Dolt (a version-controlled SQL database). It provides persistent, structured memory for coding agents, replacing markdown-based plans with a dependency-aware graph that enables agents to handle long-horizon tasks without losing context. The tool uses hash-based IDs to prevent merge collisions and supports hierarchical epics, dependency tracking, and automatic ready-work detection.

The core architecture centers on issues stored in a Dolt database with cell-level merge capabilities, supporting both local and remote sync via Dolt remotes. Beads uses a chemistry metaphor for workflow management: "protos" (templates), "molecules" (persistent work instances), and "wisps" (ephemeral operations). The system integrates with popular AI coding assistants including Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Aider, and Cursor, providing hooks for automatic context loading and task synchronization.

---

## Installation and Initialization

Install the beads CLI tool and initialize it in your project to create the `.beads/` database directory.

```bash
# Install via npm (recommended)
npm install -g @beads/bd

# Or via Homebrew
brew install beads

# Or via Go
go install github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd@latest

# Or via curl script
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

# Initialize in your project directory
cd your-project
bd init

# For open-source contributors (fork workflow)
bd init --contributor

# For team collaboration (branch workflow)
bd init --team

# Stealth mode - local only, no git operations
bd init --quiet --stealth
```

---

## Create Issues

Create new issues with title, type, priority, description, labels, and optional dependencies.

```bash
# Basic issue creation
bd create "Fix authentication bug" -t bug -p 1 -d "Users cannot log in after deployment"

# Create with labels
bd create "Add user settings page" -t feature -p 2 -l frontend,auth,ui

# Create with JSON output for programmatic use
bd create "Database migration" -t task -p 1 --json
# Output: {"id":"bd-a3f8","title":"Database migration","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",...}

# Create epic with hierarchical children
bd create "Auth System" -t epic -p 1 --json
# Returns: {"id":"bd-a3f8e9",...}

bd create "Login UI" -p 1 --parent bd-a3f8e9 --json
# Returns: {"id":"bd-a3f8e9.1",...}

bd create "Backend validation" -p 1 --parent bd-a3f8e9 --json
# Returns: {"id":"bd-a3f8e9.2",...}

# Create from markdown file
bd create -f feature-plan.md --json

# Create with dependencies
bd create "Found auth bug" -t bug -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-100 --json

# Create with external reference (link to GitHub/Jira)
bd create "Fix login" -t bug -p 1 --external-ref "gh-123" --json
```

---

## Find Ready Work

List issues that have no blocking dependencies and are ready to be worked on.

```bash
# Basic ready work query
bd ready
# Output:
# 📋 Ready work (3 issues with no blockers):
# 1. [P0] bd-a1b2: Critical security fix
# 2. [P1] bd-f3g4: Set up database
# 3. [P2] bd-h5i6: Add user settings

# With JSON output
bd ready --json
# Output: [{"id":"bd-a1b2","title":"Critical security fix","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","labels":["security"],...}]

# Filter by priority
bd ready --priority 1 --json

# Filter by label
bd ready --label backend --json

# Only unassigned issues
bd ready --unassigned --json

# Combined filters
bd ready --priority 1 --label-any frontend,backend --unassigned --json
```

---

## Claim and Update Issues

Atomically claim issues for work and update their fields.

```bash
# Atomic claim - sets assignee and status to in_progress in one operation
bd update bd-a1b2 --claim --json
# Output: {"id":"bd-a1b2","status":"in_progress","assignee":"alice",...}

# Update priority
bd update bd-a1b2 --priority 0 --json

# Update status
bd update bd-a1b2 --status blocked --json

# Update multiple fields
bd update bd-a1b2 --priority 1 --assignee bob --json

# Update multiple issues at once
bd update bd-a1b2 bd-f3g4 bd-h5i6 --priority 0 --json
```

---

## Close and Reopen Issues

Complete work by closing issues, or reopen them if needed.

```bash
# Close with reason
bd close bd-a1b2 --reason "Fixed in PR #123" --json
# Output: [{"id":"bd-a1b2","action":"closed","message":"Fixed in PR #123"}]

# Close multiple issues
bd close bd-a1b2 bd-f3g4 --reason "Batch completion" --json

# Reopen closed issues
bd reopen bd-a1b2 --reason "Need more work" --json
# Output: [{"id":"bd-a1b2","action":"reopened","message":"Need more work"}]

# Reopen multiple issues
bd reopen bd-a1b2 bd-f3g4 --json
```

---

## List and Show Issues

Query issues with filters and view detailed information.

```bash
# List open issues
bd list --status open --json

# List by type
bd list --type bug --json

# List by priority
bd list --priority 1 --json

# List by assignee
bd list --assignee alice --json

# Filter by labels (AND - must have ALL)
bd list --label backend,auth --json

# Filter by labels (OR - must have AT LEAST ONE)
bd list --label-any frontend,backend --json

# Search in title
bd list --title-contains "auth" --json

# Combined filters
bd list --status open --priority 1 --label-any urgent,critical --no-assignee --json

# Date range filters
bd list --created-after 2024-01-01 --updated-before 2024-12-31 --json

# Show detailed issue information
bd show bd-a1b2 --json
# Output: {"id":"bd-a1b2","title":"Fix auth bug","description":"...","status":"open","priority":1,"dependencies":[...],"dependents":[...],...}

# Show multiple issues
bd show bd-a1b2 bd-f3g4 --json

# Show currently active issue
bd show --current
```

---

## Dependencies

Create and manage dependency relationships between issues.

```bash
# Add blocking dependency (bd-f3g4 depends on bd-a1b2)
bd dep add bd-f3g4 bd-a1b2
# bd-f3g4 won't appear in bd ready until bd-a1b2 is closed

# Add dependency with type
bd dep add bd-f3g4 bd-a1b2 --type blocks        # Hard blocker (default)
bd dep add bd-f3g4 bd-a1b2 --type related       # Soft link, no blocking
bd dep add bd-f3g4 bd-a1b2 --type parent-child  # Epic/subtask
bd dep add bd-f3g4 bd-a1b2 --type discovered-from  # Found during work

# Remove dependency
bd dep remove bd-f3g4 bd-a1b2

# View dependency tree
bd dep tree bd-f3g4
# Output:
# 🌲 Dependency tree for bd-f3g4:
# → bd-f3g4: Add authentication [P2] (open)
#   → bd-a1b2: Create API [P2] (open)
#     → bd-h5i6: Set up database [P1] (open)

# View what's blocked
bd blocked
# Output:
# 🚫 Blocked issues:
# bd-f3g4: Add authentication
#   Blocked by: bd-a1b2 (open)

# Detect cycles
bd dep cycles
```

---

## Labels

Add, remove, and query labels for flexible categorization.

```bash
# Add labels when creating
bd create "Fix bug" -t bug -p 1 -l auth,backend,urgent

# Add label to existing issue
bd label add bd-a1b2 security
bd label add bd-a1b2 breaking-change

# Add label to multiple issues
bd label add bd-a1b2 bd-f3g4 bd-h5i6 urgent --json

# Remove label
bd label remove bd-a1b2 urgent

# List labels on issue
bd label list bd-a1b2
# Output: auth, backend, security

# List all labels in database
bd label list-all --json
# Output: [{"label":"auth","count":5},{"label":"backend","count":12},{"label":"frontend","count":8}]

# Filter by labels
bd list --label backend,auth --json        # AND - must have ALL
bd list --label-any frontend,backend --json  # OR - must have ANY
```

---

## Operational State (Labels as Cache)

Track operational state using dimension:value label patterns.

```bash
# Query current state value
bd state agent-abc patrol
# Output: active

# List all state dimensions on an issue
bd state list agent-abc --json
# Output: {"issue_id":"agent-abc","states":{"patrol":"active","mode":"normal","health":"healthy"}}

# Set state (creates event + updates label atomically)
bd set-state agent-abc patrol=muted --reason "Investigating stuck worker"

# Set multiple dimensions
bd set-state agent-abc mode=degraded --reason "High error rate"

# Query issues by state
bd list --label patrol:muted
bd list --label-any health:warning,health:failing
```

---

## Molecules and Templates

Create reusable workflow templates and instantiate them.

```bash
# List available formulas (templates)
bd formula list --json

# Show proto structure and variables
bd mol show proto-auth-flow --json

# Instantiate proto as persistent mol
bd mol pour proto-auth-flow --var user=alice --var feature=oauth --json

# Preview what would be created
bd mol pour proto-auth-flow --var user=alice --dry-run

# Instantiate as ephemeral wisp (for routine operations)
bd mol wisp proto-patrol --var target=worker-1 --json

# List all wisps
bd mol wisp list --json
bd mol wisp list --all --json  # Include closed

# Bond two work graphs together
bd mol bond epic-a epic-b --json                    # B depends on A (sequential)
bd mol bond epic-a epic-b --type parallel --json    # No blocking
bd mol bond epic-a epic-b --type conditional --json # B runs only if A fails

# Compress wisp to permanent digest
bd mol squash bd-wisp-123 --summary "Work completed" --json

# Discard wisp without record
bd mol burn bd-wisp-123 --force --json

# Garbage collect orphaned wisps
bd mol wisp gc --json
bd mol wisp gc --closed --force  # Purge all closed wisps
```

---

## Gates (External Condition Sync)

Create gates that block work until external conditions are met.

```bash
# Create PR merge gate
bd create --type=gate --title="Wait for PR #42" --await-type=gh:pr --await-id=42

# Create CI gate
bd create --type=gate --title="Wait for CI" --await-type=gh:run --await-id=12345

# Create timer gate (wait 30 minutes)
bd create --type=gate --title="Cooldown" --await-type=timer --await-id=30m

# Wire gate into dependencies
bd dep add bd-next-task gate-id

# List open gates
bd gate list
bd gate list --all  # Including closed

# Check gates (evaluate and close resolved ones)
bd gate check
bd gate check --type=gh:pr --dry-run  # Preview only PR gates

# Manually resolve a gate
bd gate resolve gate-id --reason "Approved by team lead"

# Auto-discover CI run IDs
bd gate discover --dry-run --branch main
```

---

## Sync and Remote Operations

Push and pull data to/from Dolt remotes.

```bash
# Add remote (uses git remote for refs/dolt/data)
bd dolt remote add origin git+ssh://git@github.com/org/repo.git

# Push changes to remote
bd dolt push

# Pull changes from remote
bd dolt pull

# Manual sync at end of agent session (IMPORTANT!)
bd dolt push  # Force immediate sync, bypass debounce

# Configure sync branch for protected branch workflows
bd migrate sync beads-sync
bd migrate sync beads-sync --orphan  # Create orphan branch (no shared history)
```

---

## Configuration

Set project-level and tool-level configuration.

```bash
# Set configuration value
bd config set jira.url "https://company.atlassian.net"
bd config set jira.project "PROJ"

# Get configuration value
bd config get jira.url
# Output: https://company.atlassian.net

bd config get --json jira.url
# Output: {"key":"jira.url","value":"https://company.atlassian.net"}

# List all configuration
bd config list --json

# Unset configuration
bd config unset jira.url

# Set custom statuses with categories
bd config set status.custom "in_review:active,qa_testing:wip,on_hold:frozen"

# Set custom issue types
bd config set types.custom "agent,molecule,event"

# Enable counter-based IDs instead of hash-based
bd config set issue_id_mode counter

# Configure adaptive hash ID lengths
bd config set max_collision_prob "0.25"
bd config set min_hash_length "4"
bd config set max_hash_length "8"
```

---

## Statistics and Info

Get project statistics and database information.

```bash
# Get issue statistics
bd stats
# Output:
# 📊 Statistics:
#   Total:       42
#   Open:        15
#   In Progress: 5
#   Blocked:     3
#   Closed:      19

bd stats --json
# Output: {"total":42,"open":15,"in_progress":5,"blocked":3,"closed":19,"ready":12,"avg_lead_time_hours":48.5}

# Get database and server info
bd info --json
# Output: {"database_path":"/path/to/.beads/beads.db","issue_prefix":"bd","agent_mail_enabled":false}

# Get schema info
bd info --schema --json
```

---

## Admin and Maintenance

Database maintenance, compaction, and cleanup operations.

```bash
# Clean up closed issues
bd admin cleanup --dry-run --json  # Preview
bd admin cleanup --force --json    # Delete ALL closed
bd admin cleanup --older-than 30 --force --json  # Delete closed >30 days

# Compaction (memory decay for old issues)
bd admin compact --stats --json
bd admin compact --analyze --json  # Get candidates
bd admin compact --apply --id bd-42 --summary summary.txt

# Migration after version upgrade
bd migrate --dry-run
bd migrate
bd migrate --cleanup --yes

# Reset database (remove all local data)
bd admin reset        # Preview
bd admin reset --force  # Actually reset

# Find orphan issues (referenced in git but never closed)
bd orphans --json
```

---

## Editor Integration Setup

Configure beads for various AI coding assistants.

```bash
# Claude Code
bd setup claude              # Install globally
bd setup claude --project    # Project only
bd setup claude --check      # Check status

# GitHub Copilot / Codex
bd setup codex
bd setup codex --check

# Cursor IDE
bd setup cursor
bd setup cursor --check

# Aider
bd setup aider
bd setup aider --check

# Gemini CLI
bd setup gemini
bd setup gemini --check

# Factory.ai Droid
bd setup factory

# Remove integration
bd setup claude --remove
```

---

## MCP Server Integration

The beads-mcp Python package provides Model Context Protocol (MCP) server integration for AI assistants.

```python
# Install the MCP server
pip install beads-mcp

# Run the MCP server
beads-mcp

# Example MCP tool calls (from AI assistant context)

# Set workspace context first
await context(action='set', workspace_root='/path/to/project')

# Find ready work
ready_issues = await ready(limit=5, priority=1, unassigned=True)
# Returns: [IssueMinimal(id='bd-a1b2', title='Fix bug', status='open', priority=1, ...)]

# Create issue
result = await create(title='New feature', priority=2, issue_type='feature', labels=['backend'])
# Returns: OperationResult(id='bd-f3g4', action='created')

# Claim issue
result = await claim(issue_id='bd-a1b2')
# Returns: OperationResult(id='bd-a1b2', action='claimed')

# Show issue details
issue = await show(issue_id='bd-a1b2')
# Returns: Issue(id='bd-a1b2', title='...', description='...', dependencies=[...], ...)

# Close issue
result = await close(issue_id='bd-a1b2', reason='Fixed in PR #123')
# Returns: [OperationResult(id='bd-a1b2', action='closed', message='Fixed in PR #123')]

# Add dependency
await dep(issue_id='bd-f3g4', depends_on_id='bd-a1b2', dep_type='blocks')

# Get statistics
stats = await stats()
# Returns: Stats(total=42, open=15, in_progress=5, blocked=3, closed=19, ready=12)
```

---

## Complete Agent Workflow Example

A complete workflow showing how an AI agent uses beads for task management.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Agent workflow: claim, work, discover, close, sync

# 1. Start of session - find available work
bd ready --json | jq '.'

# 2. Claim an issue atomically
bd update bd-a1b2 --claim --json

# 3. During work, discover a bug
bd create "Found edge case in validation" -t bug -p 1 \
  --deps discovered-from:bd-a1b2 \
  -l bug,backend,auth \
  --json

# 4. Add notes to current issue
bd update bd-a1b2 --notes "Investigating auth flow, found edge case" --json

# 5. Complete the original issue
bd close bd-a1b2 --reason "Implemented and tested, discovered bd-f3g4" --json

# 6. Check what got unblocked
bd blocked --json

# 7. View statistics
bd stats --json

# 8. End of session - ALWAYS sync
bd dolt push

# Optional: View dependency tree of work done
bd dep tree bd-a1b2
```

---

Beads transforms AI agent workflows by providing a structured, version-controlled task management system that eliminates the chaos of markdown-based planning. The dependency-aware graph enables agents to work autonomously across sessions, automatically detecting ready work and tracking blocked tasks. With hierarchical epics, agents can break down complex features into manageable subtasks while maintaining clear parent-child relationships.

The integration capabilities make beads particularly powerful in multi-agent environments. Teams can sync via Dolt remotes for real-time collaboration, while gates bridge external systems like GitHub PRs and CI pipelines into the dependency graph. The chemistry metaphor (protos, molecules, wisps) provides a mental model for reusable workflows, and the MCP server enables direct integration with AI assistants for seamless tool-based task management.
